audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for divorce debate, creation citation, hardness of heart, eunuchs, children, rich young man, treasure, camel/needle image, and renewal/palingenesia promise.

verses.restored: When Jesus finished these words, he departed from Galilee and came into the borders of Judea beyond the Jordan.
Great crowds followed him, and he healed them there.
Pharisees came to him, testing him and saying, Is it lawful for a man to release his wife for any cause?
He answered, Have you not read that the One who created from the beginning made them male and female,
and said, Because of this a man will leave father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two will become one flesh?
So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore what the Divine joined together, let a human not separate.
They said to him, Why then did Moses command to give a document of release and release her?
He said to them, Because of your hardness of heart Moses allowed you to release your wives; but from the beginning it has not been this way.
I say to you that whoever releases his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another commits adultery.
The disciples said to him, If such is the case of a man with his wife, it is not advantageous to marry.
He said to them, Not everyone receives this word, but only those to whom it has been given.

For there are eunuchs who were born that way from the womb, eunuchs made so by humans, and eunuchs who made themselves eunuchs because of the reign of the heavens. Let the one able to receive it receive it.
Then children were brought to him so that he might place hands on them and pray; but the disciples rebuked them.
Jesus said, Allow the children, and do not hinder them from coming to me, for of such is the reign of the heavens.
He placed his hands on them and departed from there.
And look, one came to him and said, Teacher, what good thing shall I do so that I may have age-long life?
He said to him, Why do you ask me about the good? One is good. But if you want to enter life, keep the commands.
He said to him, Which? Jesus said, You shall not murder; you shall not commit adultery; you shall not steal; you shall not bear false witness;
honor father and mother; and you shall love your neighbor as yourself.
The young man said to him, All these I have guarded. What do I still lack?
Jesus said to him, If you want to be complete/whole, go, sell your possessions and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in the heavens; then come, follow me.
When the young man heard the word, he went away grieving, for he had many possessions.
Jesus said to his disciples, Amen, I say to you: a rich person will enter the reign of the heavens with difficulty.
Again I say to you: it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the reign of the Divine.
When the disciples heard, they were greatly astonished, saying, Who then is able to be saved?
Looking at them, Jesus said, With humans this is impossible, but with the Divine all things are possible.
Then Peter answered, Look, we have left everything and followed you. What then will there be for us?

Jesus said to them, Amen, I say to you: in the renewal/regeneration, when the Son of Humanity sits on his throne of glory, you who followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
Everyone who left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or fields because of my name will receive many times more and inherit age-long life.
But many first will be last, and last first.
